{"query":"Keir Starmer resignation announcement date Labour leader 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":882,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cdxd00lg599o","title":"Keir Starmer announces resignation as prime minister and Labour Party leader","content":"His period in office will last longer than those of his Conservative predecessors Rishi Sunak and Liz Truss but will be behind all six previous Labour prime ministers.
Lord Mandelson was sacked after new information came to light about the depth of his relationship with the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Labour_Party_leadership_crisis","title":"2026 Labour Party leadership crisis - Wikipedia","content":"In 2026, a leadership crisis emerged within the Labour Party amid public and internal party dissatisfaction with Keir Starmer, the prime minister of the United Kingdom, and his government.
The Labour Party won the 2024 general election with a landslide majority of 172 seats, ousting the Conservatives after 14 years in office.
In December 2024, the government announced prominent New Labour figure Peter Mandelson's appointment as the British ambassador to the United States to strengthen their relationship with the incoming Trump administration.
By 11 May 2026, around eighty Labour MPs had called on Starmer to set a date for his departure.
On Sunday 21 June, British media was widely reporting that Starmer was expected to resign the next day.
According to LabourList, on 14 May 2026 158 Labour MPs had shown support for Starmer, 103 had called for him to resign or set out a timetable, and 143 were either unknown or had not taken a position."},{"url":"https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2026/jun/21/keir-starmer-expected-to-announce-departure-as-prime-minister-on-monday","title":"Keir Starmer expected to announce departure as prime minister on Monday | Labour party leadership | The Guardian","content":"Keir Starmer is expected to announce on Monday that he will step down as prime minister, after overwhelming pressure from Labour MPs to make way for Andy Burnham to become Labour leader.
His candidacy will become less likely if wavering Labour backbenchers conclude that they would prefer to back a likely winner and swing behind Burnham.
After the Greater Manchester mayor won Thursday’s contest by a significant margin over Reform, gaining a 9,000-plus majority and more than 50% of the vote, Burnham’s team believed they had the support of about 200 Labour MPs, about half the parliamentary party.
Starmer’s departure will set the UK on course for a seventh prime minister in 10 years, just two years after he led Labour to a sweeping general election victory, winning a majority of 174 seats."},{"url":"https://kureansiklopedi.com/en/detay/keir-starmers-resignation-2026-1ce2a","title":"Keir Starmer's Resignation (2026) | KÜRE Encyclopedia","content":"Keir Starmer announced on Monday, 22 June 2026, that he would resign.
UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces his resignation from office in front of 10 Downing Street, London, 22 June 2026 – (Anadolu Agency)
Starmer is expected to attend a NATO leaders summit on 7 July; he has indicated he will outline the military spending plan that led to Healey’s resignation on that date.
The resignation follows increasing internal party pressure after the party's electoral losses in May 2026 and Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election on 18 June."},{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/6/22/uks-starmer-expected-to-announce-exit-plan-with-burnham-on-standby","title":"Prime Minister Keir Starmer expected to announce exit plan: UK media | Politics News | Al Jazeera","content":"The threat to the British leader, which has been building for months, increased sharply on Friday when Burnham, the Greater Manchester mayor, decisively won a parliamentary election to return to Westminster, beating a candidate from Nigel Farage’s Reform UK party, which has led national opinion polls for more than a year."}]}

Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain announced his resignation on Monday, bowing to a mutiny inside his Labour Party and paving the way for Andy Burnham, the former mayor of Greater Manchester, to likely become the country’s next leader. Mr. Starmer said that he would remain as prime minister until a new party leader is selected, by September, rather than fight to remain in the job he won almost two years ago.
